habitat: Lowland forests near water; mangroves; freshwater swamp forests; secondary forests; and plantations
story: In the diverse world of birds, the Black And Red Broadbill (Cymbirhynchus macrorhynchos) stands out. This species, part of the Eurylaimidae family, is a resident of Lowland forests near water; mangroves; freshwater swamp forests; secondary forests; and plantations. Its IUCN Red List status is Least Concern (LC), which serves as a call to action for habitat preservation. The Black And Red Broadbill is more than just a bird; it is a symbol of the rich biodiversity that we must protect. Through this poster, we hope to foster a greater understanding and appreciation for the Black And Red Broadbill and its role in the ecosystem.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
